title: Intense Moroccan Egg Pilaf
description: A tangy, shaggy couscous dish cooked in a ttukbaegi with rapeseed oil, spices, preserved lemon, and eggs for a midnight craving pull.
instructions: Heat the rapeseed oil in a ttukbaegi over medium heat until shimmering.
Add the diced onion and sauté until translucent, about 5 minutes.
Stir in the minced garlic, tomato paste, cumin, paprika, and saffron, cooking for 2 minutes until fragrant.
Add the sliced preserved lemon and cook for another minute to blend the flavors.
Sprinkle the couscous over the mixture, stirring to coat the grains evenly with the sauce.
Pour in 200 ml hot water, bring the pot to a gentle boil, then reduce the heat to low, cover, and simmer for 5 minutes.
Remove the ttukbaegi from the heat, fluff the couscous with a fork, and stir in the eggs, breaking them into the hot grains.
Cover again, let the eggs steam in the residual heat for 4 minutes until set, then fluff the pilaf once more.
Garnish with chopped parsley and lemon zest, season with additional salt if desired, and serve immediately.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
